title: Error: writing blob: adding layer with blob "sha256:[ hash ]": Error processing tar file(exit status 1): operation not permitted
body:
/kind bug

**Description**

Running "podman pull docker.io/localstack/localstack" triggers this error after about 2/3 is downloaded when running podman inside another podman container.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

I have created a small repo which reproduces the bug:

https://github.com/crdoconnor/podman-in-podman-bug

I have tried it on ubuntu 22.04 and on mac M1. I saw the same results in both environments.

**Describe the results you received:**

```
Error: writing blob: adding layer with blob "sha256:[ hash ]": Error processing tar file(exit status 1): operation not permitted
```

**Describe the results you expected:**

podman pull should just work.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

4.1.1 (on mac)
3.4.4 (on ubuntu)

**Output of `podman info`:**

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md)**

Yes

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

Tried on AMD64 (ubuntu 22.04) / AARCH64 (M1).

comments.body: Can you pull *any* image, or is it just the localstack/localstack image that fails?

Some pass, some fail. The two that I verified failing multiple times were localstack/localstack and swaggerapi/swagger-ui which are exhibited via exhibitbug1.sh and exhibitbug2.sh in the repo I posted a link to. podman run hello-world will always run fine.

I'm now investigating the version of podman inside the docker container - it's running version 3.4.4 which may be why this is happening. I will try to upgrade it somehow to 4.1.1 (this seems to be tricky on ubuntu). If that fixes it I will close this issue.

Can you try using the official Podman image (`quay.io/podman/stable`)? I know we have some magic baked in there to help support Podman-in-container usecases; if it does work, it would support there being an issue with the outer container.

That's good to know.

Yeah, that seems to fix it. Shall I close the issue?
